query: iOS 27 Foundation Models framework unified Language Model protocol API documentation Claude Gemini

{"query":"iOS 27 Foundation Models framework unified Language Model protocol API documentation Claude Gemini","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1675,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2026/339/","title":"Bring an LLM provider to the Foundation Models framework - WWDC26 - Videos - Apple Developer","content":"And MLX unlocks the thousands of models available via the MLX-Community on Hugging Face.
And because these are built on top of a brand new public protocol, developers can bring frontier AI models into their apps using the same framework.
Developers can paste the URL into Xcode and start integrating your model into their apps.
How to package your LLM provider as a Swift package — configuring Package.swift with the right platform targets (iOS, macOS, visionOS, watchOS, and Linux), being deliberate about dependencies to minimize shipped bytes, and publishing a release via a git tag that developers can paste directly into Xcode."},{"url":"https://developer.apple.com/wwdc26/guides/apple-intelligence/","title":"WWDC26 Apple Intelligence guide - Apple Developer","content":"And the new App Intents Testing framework lets you validate your entire integration — Siri, Shortcuts, and Spotlight — through real system pathways, without UI automation, so you can catch issues early and ship with confidence."},{"url":"https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/cli-sdks-libraries/libraries/apple-foundation-models","title":"Apple Foundation Models - Claude Platform Docs","content":"Claude for Foundation Models is a Swift package that makes Claude available as a server-side language model in Apple's Foundation Models framework.
Constants mirror API model IDs (.opus4_8 is claude-opus-4-8) and carry each model's capabilities."},{"url":"https://blog.vibecoder.me/apple-foundation-models-claude-swift-wwdc-2026","title":"Apple Foundation Models Opens to Claude at WWDC 2026 - Apple's new LanguageModel protocol lets iOS apps swap between the on-device model and Claude with one argument change — Vibe Coder Blog","content":"Announced at WWDC 2026 on June 9 and backed by Anthropic's official ClaudeForFoundationModels Swift package, the change gives iOS and macOS developers a single LanguageModelSession API that works identically whether the model running underneath is Apple's on-device system model, Claude Sonnet, Claude Opus, or a local MLX model pulled from Hugging Face.
Now the swap is one argument.
Swapping to Claude meant scrapping the Foundation Models integration entirely and writing against the Anthropic Messages API separately.
Anthropic shipped ClaudeForFoundationModels, a Swift package that conforms Claude to this protocol, on June 8, one day before WWDC.
Google shipped an equivalent Swift package for Gemini through the same protocol.
Production apps should route through a proxy using .proxied(headers:), which sends a caller-controlled header to your backend; your backend adds the x-api-key and forwards to the Anthropic API.
Target macOS 27 or iOS 27 beta."},{"url":"https://ecorpit.com/ios-27-foundation-models-any-llm-provider-guide/","title":"iOS 27 Foundation Models: swap any LLM (2026)","content":"Apple introduced the framework at WWDC 2025 and expanded it at WWDC 2026 with an open-source utilities package, per Apple's WWDC26 session 241.
Apple's research puts the on-device model at approximately 3 billion parameters, while the third-generation server model is a 20-billion-parameter sparse design that activates 1 to 4 billion parameters at a time, per Apple Machine Learning Research.
The framework runs the system model and custom LoRA adapters you train yourself, per the Apple Developer documentation on custom adapters.
let provider: TextModelProvider = AppleOnDeviceProvider() // let provider: TextModelProvider = ClaudeProvider(apiKey: key) // let provider: TextModelProvider = GeminiProvider(apiKey: key)
_Last updated: 22 June 2026._
The Foundation Models framework is available in the Xcode 26.3 beta now, with developer and public betas running through summer 2026.
Apple's public iOS 27 release is expected in September 2026."},{"url":"https://firebase.blog/posts/2026/06/apple-foundation-models-gemini/","title":"Gemini in Apple's Foundation Models framework","content":"Jun 9, 2026 — At WWDC 2026, Apple opened the Foundation Models framework to third-party model adapters. tavily.raw: {"query":"iOS 27 Foundation Models framework unified Language Model protocol API documentation Claude Gemini","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/cli-sdks-libraries/libraries/apple-foundation-models","title":"Apple Foundation Models - Claude Platform Docs","content":"Use Claude on Apple platforms through the Foundation Models framework with the Claude for Foundation Models Swift package. Claude for Foundation Models is a Swift package that makes Claude available as a server-side language model in Apple's Foundation Models framework. The package conforms Claude to the framework's `LanguageModel` protocol, so you drive it with the same `LanguageModelSession` API you use for Apple's on-device model: `respond(to:)`, streaming, guided generation, and tool calling all work the same way. Requests go directly from your app to the Claude API; Apple is not in the request path and does not see prompts or responses. Your app decides when to use Claude and when to use Apple's on-device model: pass whichever model you want to each session. **Beta.** This package targets the Foundation Models server-side language model API introduced in the OS 27 betas. Claude for Foundation Models is **not** a general-purpose Messages API client. * A Claude API key from the Claude Console for development.","score":0.78846955,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://github.com/anthropics/ClaudeForFoundationModels","title":"GitHub - anthropics/ClaudeForFoundationModels: Claude support for Apple Foundation Models · GitHub","content":"You signed in with another tab or window. # Claude for Foundation Models. Use Claude as a server-side language model through Apple's Foundation Models framework. The package conforms Claude to the framework's `LanguageModel` protocol, so you drive it with the same `LanguageModelSession` API you use for Apple's on-device model — `respond(to:)`, streaming, guided generation, and tool calling all work the same way. > **Beta.** This package targets the Foundation Models server-side language model API introduced in the OS 27 betas. Pass it to `LanguageModelSession` and use the session exactly as you would with any Foundation Models provider. Use a compiled-in constant, or construct one with explicit capabilities for an ID that isn't compiled in yet (see Capabilities):. Constants mirror API model IDs (`.opus4_8` is `claude-opus-4-8`) and carry each model's capabilities. For an ID that isn't compiled in, declare what the model accepts:. The framework's reasoning levels map to effort per request: `.light` → `low`, `.moderate` → `medium`, `.deep` → `high`, and `.custom` accepts a Claude effort name directly (`\"xhigh\"`, `\"max\"`).","score":0.77137923,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.fonearena.com/blog/484623/apple-wwdc26-foundation-models-gemini-support-xcode-27-agentic-coding-tools.html/amp","title":"Apple WWDC26: Foundation Models gain Gemini support, Xcode 27 gets ...","content":"Developers can also use models such as Gemini, Claude, and other services that implement the LanguageModel protocol. Developers enrolled in the","score":0.6320719,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://rits.shanghai.nyu.edu/ai/apple-open-sources-its-foundation-models-framework-adds-claude-and-gemini","title":"Apple Open-Sources Its Foundation Models Framework, Adds Claude and Gemini","content":"# Apple Open-Sources Its Foundation Models Framework, Adds Claude and Gemini. **At WWDC 2026 on June 9, Apple announced it will open-source its Foundation Models framework** — the on-device AI layer that powers Apple Intelligence — later this summer. Alongside the open-source pledge, Apple opened the framework to third-party models like Claude and Gemini, added image input, and made its newest cloud models free to small developers on Private Cloud Compute. The headline for developers is that the Foundation Models framework — the native Swift API Apple shipped in 2025 for tapping its on-device models — will go open source later this summer. ## The Models Behind It. Powering all of this is Apple’s third generation of foundation models (AFM 3), a five-model lineup spanning on-device and cloud. Diagram of Apple's third-generation Foundation Models lineup spanning on-device and cloud models. For developers, it means one Swift API can route between a private on-device model, Apple’s free cloud tier, or a frontier model like Claude — chosen per request based on cost, latency, and privacy.","score":0.5832277,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://developer.apple.com/documentation/foundationmodels","title":"Foundation Models | Apple Developer Documentation","content":"# Foundation Models. Perform tasks with models that specialize in language understanding, structured output, and tool calling. The Foundation Models framework provides access to any large language model, like the on-device and Private Cloud Compute models designed for Apple Intelligence. An illustration that represents a foundation model. On-device models excel at a diverse range of text generation tasks, like summarization, entity extraction, text and image understanding, refinement, dialog for games, generating creative content, and more. With the `@Generable` macro, you can define custom data structures and the framework provides strong guarantees that the model generates instances of your type. Use `Tool` to create custom tools that the model can call to assist with handling your request. For example, the model can call a tool that searches a local or online database for information, or calls a service in your app. To use Apple Foundation Models, people need to turn on Apple Intelligence on their device. Build agentic app experiences with the Foundation Models framework.","score":0.57293844,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.57,"request_id":"5d19957f-7fa2-43df-9536-53a22d767ea6"}
